center.name: 普诺大区
center.summary: 普诺大区是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联41种鸟类，包括高山斑䳍、翎冠刺尾蜂鸟、秘鲁斑尾蜂鸟等41种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: peruviana: Andes of western and southern Peru from Ancash to Arequipa and Puno departments, and into Bolivia as far as La Paz Department The Andean tit-spinetail generally inhabits semi-arid to arid montane scrublands

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in the Andes from southwestern Táchira in extreme western Venezuela into northern Colombia, in all three Andean ranges in Colombia, through Ecuador on both slopes, and into Peru on the eastern slope as far as Puno Department

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: signatus: eastern slope of Andes from northern Ecuador's Napo Province south and intermittently through Peru to northern Puno Department The ashy-throated chlorospingus inhabits lowland and tropical evergreen forest and tropical deciduous forest. It favors wet forest heavy with moss and is found mostly in the forest canopy and its edges. It ranges in elevation between in Costa Rica, between in Colombia, mostly between in western Ecuador and between in the east, mostly between in Peru but only at about in Tumbes, and between in Venezuela

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In Peru it occurs in the departments of Ucayali, Cuzco, Madre de Dios, and Puno

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: meridionalis is found in Peru on the western Andean slope from Ecuador south to Cajamarca Department and on the eastern slope from southern Amazonas Department south to northern Puno Department

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in Peru primarily in Arequipa and Puno departments and very rarely as far north as Ancash Department.{{cite book | last =Schulenberg | first =T.S

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: caesius: from southeastern Peru's Puno Department into west-central Bolivia's La Paz and Cochabamba departments The black-throated tody-tyrant inhabits humid montane forest, cloudforest, and shrubby regenerating areas in the subtropical and temperate zones

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: berlepschi: from Cuzco and Puno departments in southeastern Peru into central Bolivia's La Paz and Cochabamba departments The brown-backed chat-tyrant primarily inhabits stunted montane forest up to tree line, Polylepis woodlands, and shrubby paramo

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The brownish-headed antbird is found in the southeastern Peruvian departments of Cuzco, Madre de Dios, and Puno and in west-central La Paz Department in far west-central Bolivia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate subspecies is found in central and southern Peru from the Department of Huánuco south into the Department of Puno; it might also occur in the western part of the Department of Ucayali

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in Peru from La Libertad and San Martín departments south to Cuzco and northern Puno departments

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: poliophrys: Andes of central and southern Peru from the Department of Huánuco to the Department of Puno The grey-browed brushfinch inhabits the interior and edges of humid montane forest and secondary forest. It ranges in elevation between in Venezuela, between in Colombia, between in the northern part of Ecuador, and between and locally much lower in the southern part.{{cite book | last1 =McMullan | first1 =Miles | last2 =Donegan | first2 =Thomas M

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: bolivianum is found in eastern Peru from Ucayali Department south into northern Puno Department,{{cite book | last =Schulenberg | first =T.S

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate subspecies of the light-crowned spinetail is found in the Andes of the Department of Puno in far southern Peru and in northern Bolivia's La Paz and Cochabamba departments

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate subspecies is found in the Andes from the departments of Cuzco and Puno in Peru south into western Bolivia as far as La Paz and Cochabamba departments

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: boliviana: from Pasco in central Peru southeast to Puno Department and into central Bolivia as far as Cochabamba Department The ochre-breasted antpitta inhabits the undergrowth of humid to wet montane forest in the foothill and subtropical zones

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: squamiger: the Andes from southern Peru's Department of Puno south into Bolivia as far as Santa Cruz Department A single sight record in northern Argentina's Salta Province leads the South American Classification Committee of the American Ornithological Society to call the species hypothetical in that country. The pearled treerunner primarily inhabits montane evergreen forest and elfin forest, and also occurs locally in Polylepis woodland and secondary forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The plumbeous tyrant is found in the Andes from eastern Cuzco and northern Puno departments in southern Peru south through Bolivia into northwestern Argentina as far as southeastern Catamarca Province

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: rufipectoralis: from Peru's southeastern Cuzco and northern Puno departments into western Bolivia as far as western Santa Cruz Department The rufous-breasted chat-tyrant primarily inhabits the edges of montane forest, cloudforest, and Polylepis woodlands in the temperate zone

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: https://www.museum.lsu.edu/~Remsen/SACCCountryLists.htm retrieved July 28, 2024 In Bolivia it occurs from far west-central Santa Cruz Department northwest through Cochabamba Department and La Paz Department. According to the International Ornithological Committee its range extends from La Paz slightly into southeastern Peru's Department of Puno. The species seldom occurs in the interior of mature forest but inhabits areas with dense vegetation such as secondary forest and forest edges along roads and watercourses

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: hypochlorus: Peru from Cuzco Department south to Puno Department; (also Bolivia) The scale-crested pygmy tyrant inhabits humid to wet forest and mature secondary forest; it also occurs at the forest edges and in gaps such as those caused by fallen trees

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: subtropicalis is found in southeastern Peru in Cuzco and Puno departments

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: One population is found in the Andes of southern Peru's Department of Puno and west-central Bolivia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: griseipectus is found in the southern Peruvian departments of Apurímac, Cuzco, and Puno

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: pallidus is found in the Andes from Cuzco and Puno departments in Peru south through Bolivia into northwestern Argentina as far as Tucumán Province.{{cite book | last =Hilty | first =Steven L

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: fresnayana: far southeastern Peru's Department of Puno and northwestern and central Bolivia The white-flanked antwren inhabits the understorey and mid-storey of evergreen forest including terra firme, várzea, igapó, and transitional types

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The white-lined antbird is found in southeastern Peru from the Department of Ucayali south to the Department of Puno, in adjoining far western Acre state of Brazil, and in northwestern Bolivia's Pando and La Paz departments

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: axillaris: east slope of the Andes of southeastern Peru between the departments of Cuzco and Puno The ranges of puncticeps and axillaris have a gap between them. The yellow-breasted antwren inhabits the interior and edges of humid evergreen forest, where it tends to favor the canopy

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It occurs in southeastern Peru from eastern Cuzco Department through Puno Department and possibly Madre de Dios Department into western Bolivia where its range continues through La Paz Department into Cochabamba Department

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate subspecies is found in the far northern end of Colombia's Western Andes, on both slopes of the Central Andes in Antioquia Department, and on the eastern slope of the Eastern Andes from Boyacá Department south. Its range continues south on the eastern Andean slope through Ecuador and intermittently in Peru to Puno Department.{{cite book | last =Schulenberg | first =T.S

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: aureiventris: eastern slope of the Andes in Peru from Huánuco Department south to Puno Department The ornate flycatcher inhabits humid montane forest, mature secondary forest, and woodlands in the Andean foothills

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: alticola is found in the Altiplano of central and southeastern Peru from Junín Department south through Puno Department into western Bolivia' La Paz and Oruro departments and northwestern Argentina as far as Tucumán Province

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The upland antshrike is found in the Andean foothills from the Department of Puno in extreme southeastern Peru southeast to Cochabamba and western Santa Cruz departments in Bolivia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in southeastern Peru's Cuzco and Puno departments, across northern Bolivia, and in Brazil south of the Amazon east to the Tapajós River

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The subspecies are found thus: *Thamnophilus ruficapillus jaczewskii: Andes of northern Peru in the departments of Cajamarca, Amazonas, and San Martín *Thamnophilus ruficapillus marcapatae: eastern slope of the Andes of southern Peru in Cuzco and Puno departments *Thamnophilus ruficapillus subfasciatus: eastern slope of the Andes of northwestern Bolivia's La Paz and western Cochabamba departments *Thamnophilus ruficapillus cochabambae: eastern slope of the Andes from eastern Cochabamba in Bolivia southeast through Santa Cruz Department into northwestern Argentina as far as Tucumán Province *Thamnophilus ruficapillus ruficapillus: southeastern Brazil from eastern Minas Gerais and Espírito Santo south and west into eastern Paraguay, northeastern Argentina as far as Buenos Aires Province, and Uruguay The rufous-capped antshrike inhabits a variety of landscapes that differ geographically

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: cyanolaema is found in southern Peru from Ayacucho Department south to Cuzco and Puno departments.Hosner, P

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It occurs in the east Andean foothills of Huánuco, Pasco, Cuzco, and Puno departments

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The wire-crested thorntail is found in the Andean foothills from eastern Colombia's Meta Department through eastern Ecuador into eastern Peru as far south as the Department of Puno

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate subspecies of the rusty-winged barbtail is found in all three Andean ranges of Colombia and south through the Andes of Ecuador into those of Peru as far as the Department of Puno

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: omissa is found in the Peruvian Andes between Cuzco and Puno departments

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: (2008) | range_map = Nothoprocta taczanowskii map.svg }} Taczanowski's tinamou (Nothoprocta taczanowskii) is a type of ground bird found in the eastern Andes in Peru in the Junín, Cuzco, Apurímac, Ayacucho, and Puno Regions.Clements, J
